g.text: Premium Creator Workstation V1 - Importable SVG Mockup
Static SVG for Figma import. Visual direction only. No unsupported cloud, collaboration, analytics, fake timeline, fake AI chat, or fake one-click publish capabilities.
AI-assisted local rendering intelligence for creator clips.
No fake cloud, timeline, analytics, collaboration, or AI chat.
Unsupported capability appears only in Out of Scope.
Create a browser-safe preview session before entering Studio.
session_id, duration, title, preview URL, export_dir
Desktop folder picker optional; browser fallback allowed
Maps to POST /api/render/prepare-source and preview endpoints.
Preview anchored. Controls are grouped by creator intent, not backend file structure.
subtitle, text, crop and voice state preview
advisory by default; execution influence is advanced opt-in
Motion crop may fall back safely
Studio builds the draft; Monitor owns the job after submit.
Progress first. Logs and diagnostics remain secondary.
Stage: rendering_parallel. Transport: WebSocket live, polling fallback available.
If interrupted, resume and retry actions appear here.
Structured render events remain support detail.
The hero product moment: ranked outputs, reasons, warnings, summaries, and export.
Hook, retention, speech density, quality warnings.
Advisory, applied, skipped, blocked only when metadata exists.
Narration applied, translation not used, subtitles valid.
Show successful outputs first. Retry supported failures.
History is organized by real job status, not a cloud project library.
5 outputs, best clip available, AI explanation present
3 outputs saved, 2 failed parts can be reviewed
Monitor can rebuild state from job and part rows
Open Results, Monitor, Retry, Rerun, Open Folder
No collaboration, no cloud project library.
Separate from render preview sessions. No AI download intelligence.
Advanced only. Real accounts, videos, queue, scheduler, proxies, worker state. No fake publish guarantee.
login checks and proxy tests are explicit operations
manual files or render export later references
run, hold, resume, cancel, retry failed
Runtime health belongs here, with contextual warnings elsewhere.
Whisper available; model may warm on first use
Desktop adapter: folder picker, open path, browser fallback
Creator clip ranking decisions or render draft controls.
Terminal and partial states must be visible. Missing optional metadata must never crash UI.
Partial success keeps successful outputs primary. Failed parts move into recovery.
AI unavailable is not a render failure. Missing optional metadata omits panels or shows unavailable state.
Source - prepare-source response and preview URLs
Monitor - status, stage, progress, queue, logs, retry/resume
Results - outputs, ranking, best clip, failed parts, summaries, AI metadata
Downloads - batch URL jobs and retry behavior
System - warmup, diagnostics, desktop adapter capability
Parse result_json once. Preserve aliases. Optional AI metadata cannot crash UI.
WebSocket accelerates state. Polling remains authoritative fallback.
These must not appear as real product features in screens.
No cloud sync or cloud rendering
No multi-user collaboration, teams, roles, billing, or permissions
No social analytics dashboard or campaign management
No full nonlinear timeline or arbitrary clip sequencing
No AI prompt box for arbitrary video editing
No AI rewriting arbitrary FFmpeg commands or inventing timestamps
No official platform API publishing or guaranteed upload success
No guaranteed perfect translation, mastering, or cinematic crop
No mobile-first app and no current UI visual reuse
Rule: if backend does not implement it, do not design it.
